chunk_summary: This section outlines the micronutrient supplementation schedule for inpatient SAM care, emphasizing proper dosing, timing, and integration with feeding protocols during stabilization and early rehabilitation phases.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of therapeutic foods (F-75, F-100, RUTF) in micronutrient delivery for SAM patients.
Learn the standard inpatient supplementation schedule, including folic acid, zinc, copper, and multivitamins.
Differentiate between routine supplementation and condition-triggered therapies like iron and high-dose vitamin A.
Recognize the importance of linking supplementation to feeding delivery and monitoring adherence.
key_facts: Folic acid: 5 mg on day 1, followed by 1 mg daily.
Zinc: 2 mg/kg/day and copper: 0.3 mg/kg/day for at least 2 weeks.
Multivitamin preparations should include vitamin B complex, vitamin C, and vitamin D.
Micronutrients should be given with feeds to reduce gastric intolerance.
Iron and high-dose vitamin A require specific timing and indications in SAM care.
reference_quote: Micronutrient therapy in SAM must be prescribed with attention to what the child is actually receiving. If therapeutic foods are correctly prepared and meet specifications (F-75, F-100, or RUTF), additional multivitamins and trace elements may be unnecessary and can cause duplication.
